---
title: Visualization and residualization
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Get structural and correlation networks

```{python}

for h in range(2):
    match h:
        case 0:
            W = data["W"]
        case 1:
            W = data["X"].T @ data["X"]
            Xg = abct.residualn(data["X"].T, "global").T
            Xg = Xg / np.linalg.norm(Xg, axis=0, keepdims=True)
    
    ND = dict()
    ND["Original"] = W
    ND["Degree-corrected"] = abct.residualn(W, "degree")
    match h:
        case 0: ND["Rank-one subtracted"] = abct.residualn(W, "rankone")
        case 1: ND["Global-signal regressed"] = Xg.T @ Xg
    ND["Co-neighbor"] = abct.kneighbor(W, "common", kappa=0.1).toarray()
    ND["Nearest-neighbor"] = abct.kneighbor(W, "nearest", kappa=0.1).toarray()
    match h:
        case 0: WD = ND.copy()
        case 1: CD = ND.copy()

```

### Visualize structural and correlation networks

```{python}

for h in range(2):
    match h:
        case 0:
            ND = WD
            ord = data["ordw"].flatten() - 1
            cmap = "inferno"
            zmin = (0, -0.005, -0.005, 0, 0)
            zmax = (0.01, 0.005, 0.005, 36, 1)
            tlab = "structural networks"
        case 1:
            ND = CD
            ord = data["ordc"].flatten() - 1
            cmap = "viridis"
            zmin = (0, -0.2, -0.5, 0, 0)
            zmax = (1, 0.2, 0.5, 36, 1)
            tlab = "correlation networks"

    for i, name in enumerate(ND):
        fig = px.imshow(
            ND[name][np.ix_(ord, ord)],
            color_continuous_scale=cmap,
            zmin=zmin[i],
            zmax=zmax[i],
        )
        fig.update_layout(
            title=f"{name} {tlab}",
            title_x=0.5,
            width=600,
            height=600,
        )
        fig.show()

```

### Visualize residual structural and correlation networks

```{python}

neye = ~np.eye(len(data["W"]), dtype=bool) 

# Scatter plot of degree-corrected and rank-one subtracted structural networks
for h in range(2):
    match h:
        case 0:
            x = WD["Rank-one subtracted"][neye]
            y = WD["Degree-corrected"][neye]
            xlab = "Network weights after first-component removal"
            ylab = "Network weights after degree correction"
            tlab = "Structural networks"
        case 1:
            x = CD["Global-signal regressed"][neye]
            y = CD["Degree-corrected"][neye]
            xlab = "Network weights after degree correction"
            ylab = "Network weights after global-signal regression"
            tlab = "Correlation networks"

    r = np.corrcoef(x, y)[0, 1]
    fig = px.scatter(x=x, y=y)
    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title=xlab,
        yaxis_title=ylab,
        title=f"{tlab} (r = {r:.3f})",
        title_x=0.5,
        width=600,
        height=600,
    )
    fig.show()

```
